reader_visibility.note: Only `stance` reaches the reader, and only indirectly — as the argument behind a Research Angle or a 'why we care'. `confidence`, `falsifier`, `drift_log` and `candidate_evidence` are internal: machine tripwires plus a developer inspection surface. Never render them in a published issue.

degradation_contract.on_empty_stance: Pipeline runs to completion. Thesis-dependent sections (Research Angle, 'why we care') render the marker below instead of an opinion. Researchers and manager MUST NOT improvise a stance to fill the gap.
degradation_contract.rationale: A missing thesis must bend the output, not break the run. An unowned opinion is worse than a visible gap.

self_evolution.rule: A slot with an empty stance is DORMANT: the loop must not author a stance for it. Each cycle, a dormant slot may only append to `candidate_evidence` — sourced items that would bear on the belief once a human writes it. A slot activates the moment its stance is non-empty; from then on the loop may revise stance/confidence and must append every revision to `drift_log`.

belief_slot_contract.id: stable kebab-case key; never reused after deletion
belief_slot_contract.title: human-readable belief area
belief_slot_contract.stance: string | null — the opinionated, falsifiable position. Human-authored at seeding. null = dormant.
belief_slot_contract.falsifier: string | null — what observation would force revision. Internal only; the loop checks it each cycle.
belief_slot_contract.candidate_evidence: array of {date, headline, url, slot_relevance} — accumulated while dormant
belief_slot_contract.drift_log: array of {date, from_stance, to_stance, trigger, cycle_id} — append-only, populated only after activation
belief_slot_contract.origin: 'seed' | 'auto' — auto slots are added by the loop when a recurring theme has no home
